title: Psoriasis: Causes, Medicinal Herbs and Herbal Preparations
definition: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune condition that causes red, scaly patches to appear on the skin. While these physical symptoms can be uncomfortable and embarrassing, the impact of psoriasis extends far beyond its visible effects. The constant itching, burning, and stinging sensations can disrupt sleep patterns, leading to fatigue, irritability, and decreased productivity at work or school. Social interactions become challenging as individuals with psoriasis may feel self-conscious about their appearance, avoiding social events, intimate relationships, or even simple activities like swimming or hiking. The emotional toll of living with psoriasis can also lead to depression, anxiety, and low self-esteem, making it difficult to maintain a sense of normalcy in daily life. Additionally, the condition's impact on mental health can affect relationships, career advancement, and overall well-being, making it essential for individuals with psoriasis to find effective treatment options and support networks to manage their symptoms and improve their quality of life.
definition_desc: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches to appear on the skin. These patches can range from small to large and can appear anywhere on the body, but are commonly found on the elbows, knees, and scalp. Psoriasis not only affects a person's physical appearance, but it also has a significant impact on their quality of life. For example, individuals with psoriasis may experience feelings of embarrassment and self-consciousness, which can lead to social isolation and depression. They may also struggle with painful skin cracks and bleeding, making everyday activities like bathing and dressing a challenge. Additionally, psoriasis can also make it difficult to engage in hobbies and activities that require physical exertion, such as playing sports or going for long walks. Furthermore, the constant itching and scratching can disrupt sleep patterns, leading to fatigue and decreased productivity. In some cases, psoriasis can also lead to skin infections, which can further complicate the condition and require medical treatment. Overall, psoriasis can have a profound impact on a person's daily life, making even simple tasks a daunting challenge.
intro_desc: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin condition characterized by the rapid growth of skin cells, leading to red, scaly patches and inflammation. Its causes include genetic predisposition, immune system dysregulation, and environmental triggers such as stress, smoking, and certain medications. If left untreated, psoriasis can lead to emotional distress, compromised skin integrity, and increased risk of infections and cardiovascular disease. Herbal remedies, such as aloe vera, turmeric, and tea tree oil, have been found to soothe and calm the skin, while preparations like oatmeal baths and calendula creams can help manage symptoms. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using medicinal herbs, as some may interact with medications or exacerbate underlying conditions.
causes_desc: The main causes of psoriasis are multifactorial, involving a combination of genetic, environmental, and immune system factors. Research suggests that 40-50% of individuals with psoriasis have a family history of the disease, indicating a strong genetic component. Additionally, individuals with a history of infections, particularly strep throat, can trigger psoriasis onset or exacerbation. Imbalances in the immune system, including overproduction of pro-inflammatory cytokines, also contribute to the development of psoriasis. Other risk factors include smoking, obesity, and stress, which can all exacerbate the disease.

causes_list.cause_description: Genetics are a primary cause of psoriasis as individuals with a family history of psoriasis are more likely to develop the condition due to inherited genetic mutations that affect the immune system and skin cell growth.

Imbalanced gut bacteria, particularly an overgrowth of certain bacteria such as Klebsiella and an undergrowth of beneficial bacteria, can trigger an immune response and lead to the development of psoriasis.
Stress can trigger psoriasis by releasing inflammatory chemicals that stimulate the immune system and lead to the production of skin cells, which can cause skin cell buildup and plaque formation.
A weakened immune system, often caused by a lack of vitamin D, can lead to an overactive immune response and the development of psoriasis.
Certain infections, such as strep throat and skin infections, can trigger psoriasis by stimulating the immune system and leading to inflammation and skin cell buildup.
Medications such as beta-blockers, lithium, and certain antimalarial drugs can trigger psoriasis by disrupting the normal functioning of the skin and immune system.
Smoking can trigger psoriasis by reducing blood flow to the skin, leading to inflammation and skin cell buildup.
Obesity can trigger psoriasis by causing inflammation and releasing inflammatory chemicals that stimulate the immune system and lead to the development of psoriasis.
Hormonal changes, such as those experienced during pregnancy, menopause, or thyroid disorders, can trigger psoriasis by disrupting the normal functioning of the skin and immune system.
A vitamin D deficiency can lead to an overactive immune response and the development of psoriasis by disrupting the normal functioning of the skin and immune system.
Environmental factors, such as cold weather, dry air, and exposure to chemicals and pollutants, can trigger psoriasis by disrupting the normal functioning of the skin and immune system.

A diet high in processed foods, sugar, and dairy products can trigger psoriasis by causing inflammation and releasing inflammatory chemicals that stimulate the immune system and lead to the development of psoriasis.

herbs_desc: The main medicinal herbs used for psoriasis are Urtica dioica, Aloe vera, Taraxacum officinale, Silybum marianum, Avena sativa, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba. Urtica dioica, rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, has been shown to reduce inflammation and suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating psoriatic symptoms. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, combined with its ability to promote wound healing, make it an effective treatment for psoriasis. Taraxacum officinale and Silybum marianum are rich in saponins and flavonoids, respectively, which have been found to reduce oxidative stress and modulate the immune response in individuals with psoriasis. Additionally, Avena sativa, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Ginkgo biloba have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antiproliferative, and antioxidant properties, which can help to manage psoriasis symptoms and promote skin health.

herbs_list.description: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition.
Aloe vera helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may reduce redness and itching, and promote wound healing in affected skin.

Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, helps with psoriasis due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the condition.

Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, helps with psoriasis due to its antioxidant properties, which may reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the condition, and its potential to improve liver function.

Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may reduce redness and itching, and promote relaxation and reduce stress, a common trigger for the condition.

Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may reduce redness and itching, and promote wound healing in affected skin.
Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may reduce redness and itching, and promote wound healing in affected skin.
Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, helps with psoriasis due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which may reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition.

Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, helps with psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may reduce redness and itching, and promote wound healing in affected skin.

Ginkgo biloba has not been shown to have a direct effect on psoriasis, but may help with the condition due to its antioxidant properties, which may reduce oxidative stress associated with the condition.

preparations_desc: The main herbal preparations used for psoriasis are Cream, Decoction, Salve, Tincture, Ointment, Capsule, Gel, Poultice, Suppository, Tea. Cream, typically containing aloe vera or capsaicin, helps soothe and reduce inflammation. Decoction of herbs such as turmeric and ginger has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce psoriasis symptoms. Salve, ointment, and gel preparations, often containing omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin D, or aloe vera, provide moisturizing and anti-inflammatory effects. Tincture, capsule, poultice, suppository, and tea preparations of herbs like tea tree oil, licorice root, and stinging nettle may also offer anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory benefits for psoriasis management.
precautions_desc: The main precautions to take before using herbal remedies for psoriasis relief are to consult a healthcare professional, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications, as some herbs can interact with them. Additionally, be aware of potential allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or anaphylaxis, and monitor your skin closely for any adverse effects. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort and ginkgo biloba, can exacerbate psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, so use them with caution. Also, be cautious of herbal remedies that contain high levels of vitamin A, which can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications. Lastly, ensure the herbal remedy is of high quality and manufactured by a reputable company to minimize the risk of contamination or adulteration.

preparations_list.preparation_description: Teas are used to reduce inflammation and promote healing in psoriasis by releasing anti-inflammatory compounds into the bloodstream, which then help to soothe and calm the affected skin.

Decoctions are used to deliver beneficial compounds to the skin and reduce inflammation in psoriasis by releasing these compounds slowly over time, allowing for a prolonged period of relief from symptoms.

Tinctures are used to reduce pain and inflammation in psoriasis by delivering a concentrated dose of anti-inflammatory compounds directly to the affected skin, where they can exert their therapeutic effects.
Salves are used to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin in psoriasis by creating a protective barrier on the skin's surface that prevents water loss and reduces inflammation.

Creams are used to moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin in psoriasis by delivering a rich, hydrating dose of emollients and humectants directly to the affected skin, where they can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Ointments are used to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin in psoriasis by creating a thick, protective barrier on the skin's surface that prevents water loss and reduces inflammation.

Capsules are used to deliver beneficial compounds systemically in psoriasis, allowing for a more rapid and intense therapeutic effect, as the compounds can be absorbed quickly into the bloodstream and reach the affected skin directly.

Tablets are used to deliver beneficial compounds systemically in psoriasis, allowing for a more rapid and intense therapeutic effect, as the compounds can be absorbed quickly into the bloodstream and reach the affected skin directly.

Suppositories are used to deliver beneficial compounds directly to the affected skin in psoriasis, particularly in areas where creams or ointments may not be effective, such as the genital or anal areas.
Compresses are used to reduce inflammation and promote healing in psoriasis by applying a warm, wet dressing directly to the affected skin, which helps to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation.

precautions_list.precaution_description: Consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies for psoriasis, especially if you're taking medications or have underlying health conditions, to ensure safe and effective use.
Monitor your skin closely for any signs of adverse reactions or worsening of psoriasis symptoms while using herbal remedies, and discontinue use if you experience any unusual or severe reactions.
Use high-quality herbal preparations that have been standardized for their active compounds and are manufactured by reputable companies to ensure efficacy and safety.
Follow the recommended dosage and preparation guidelines for herbal remedies to avoid overuse, underuse, or interactions with other medications.

Be aware of potential interactions between herbal remedies and other medications, such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, or immunosuppressants, and consult a healthcare professional if you have concerns.

Watch for allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, or difficulty breathing, when using herbal remedies for psoriasis, and seek medical attention if you experience any severe or anaphylactic reactions.
Report any changes in psoriasis symptoms, such as worsening of symptoms or appearance of new lesions, to your healthcare professional while using herbal remedies to adjust treatment as needed.
Be patient and consistent with herbal remedy use, as it may take several weeks or months to see noticeable improvements in psoriasis symptoms.
Keep records of your herbal remedy use, including dosages, preparation methods, and any adverse reactions, to help monitor effectiveness and safety.

related_ailments_desc: If you have psoriasis, there's a high chance you also experience joint pain and stiffness due to psoriatic arthritis, an inflammatory condition that affects the joints, causing redness and swelling in the affected areas. You may also be at risk of developing eye problems such as conjunctivitis, uveitis and even vision loss, due to inflammation in the eyes. Additionally, you may be more prone to developing cardiovascular conditions like high blood pressure, heart attack and stroke due to chronic inflammation in the body. You may also be at risk of developing kidney disease and kidney stones due to the buildup of uric acid in the kidneys. Furthermore, you may experience fatigue and weakness due to anemia, which can be caused by the buildup of iron in the body.

related_ailments_list.ailment_description: People with psoriasis often experience arthritis due to inflammation of the joints caused by an abnormal immune response that also triggers psoriasis symptoms.
Individuals with psoriasis have a higher risk of developing diabetes, particularly type 2, due to chronic inflammation and insulin resistance associated with the disease.

Psoriasis patients are at an increased risk of cardiovascular disease, including heart attacks, strokes, and high blood pressure, due to inflammation and atherosclerosis that can lead to cardiovascular events.
The physical symptoms of psoriasis, social stigma, and chronic pain can contribute to depression in people with the disease, making it a common comorbidity.
The stress of living with a chronic condition like psoriasis, coupled with physical symptoms and social stigma, can lead to anxiety disorders in affected individuals.
Psoriasis patients often experience weight gain and obesity due to inflammation-driven changes in metabolism and appetite regulation, as well as reduced mobility and physical activity.
Chronic inflammation and kidney damage can lead to kidney disease in individuals with psoriasis, particularly those with severe or long-standing disease.
Psoriasis patients are at risk of developing eye problems, such as conjunctivitis, uveitis, and vision loss, due to inflammation and immune system dysregulation that can affect the eyes.
Chronic inflammation and liver damage can contribute to liver disease, including non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), in individuals with psoriasis.

The chronic inflammation associated with psoriasis can lead to bone loss and osteoporosis, particularly in individuals with long-standing or severe disease, making them more susceptible to fractures and osteoporotic-related complications.

preparation_list.description: A cream preparation of the herb Calendula officinalis helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition.

A decoction of the roots of the herb Echinacea purpurea helps with psoriasis by its immunomodulatory properties, which stimulate the immune system to fight off infections and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of the condition.

A salve preparation of the herb Plantago major helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition and preventing infection.

A tincture of the herb Aloe barbadensis helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition, and promoting wound healing.

An ointment preparation of the herb Cetraria islandica helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition, and promoting skin hydration.

A capsule preparation of the herb Turmeric (Curcuma longa) helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition, and alleviate symptoms such as redness and itching.

A gel preparation of the herb Aloe barbadensis helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition, and promoting wound healing.

A poultice of the herb Plantago major helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition, and preventing infection.

A suppository preparation of the herb Calendula officinalis helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which soothe and protect the affected area, reducing redness and inflammation associated with the condition, and preventing infection.

A tea preparation of the herb Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) helps with psoriasis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety associated with the condition, and promoting relaxation.
